ds: A haunted castle. A missing heir. And something that should have stayed buried. Pre-generated level 5 characters provided. This gothic horror one-shot blends investigation with deadly combat as your party unravels the curse of Castle Aldric. Content warning: body horror, undead. New players welcome.

A Pathfinder Society scenario set in the Five Kings Mountains. Your faction has uncovered evidence of a duergar weapons cache threatening Highhelm's defenses. Tier 3-6. Bring your organized play character or use a pre-gen. Reporting for PFS credit.

An apartment building where the geometry doesn't work. Doors open to rooms that shouldn't exist. Pre-generated agents provided. This is a slow-burn psychological horror scenario from the award-winning Impossible Landscapes campaign. Mature themes: paranoia, reality distortion, existential dread. 5 hours to unravel something that cannot be understood.

Your ship is becalmed in cursed waters. Something is rising from below. OSR rules-light pirate horror. Pre-gens provided. New players welcome. Expect character death, dark humor, and creative problem-solving.
Adventurers League Season 26 premiere! The town of Phandalin is threatened by a gathering storm. Tier 1 (levels 1-4). Bring your AL character or use a pre-gen. Official AL reporting.

Play the officially released version of Daggerheart from Critical Role's Darrington Press! The Ember Vault holds a secret that could reshape the world. Pre-generated characters. No experience necessary. Materials and dice provided.

The classic introductory scenario, run by Chaosium's own organized play team. A seemingly simple investigation into a haunted house in Boston becomes something much darker. Pre-generated investigators. Perfect for first-time CoC players — our GMs are trained to teach the system while delivering genuine cosmic dread.

A distress signal from a mining colony. Standard colonial marines stuff, right? Mothership's panic mechanic will have you making bad decisions under pressure. Pre-gen marines. Sci-fi survival horror — tension, resource scarcity, creative deaths. Content: claustrophobia, body horror.

t: Blades in the Dark: The Silver Rail Job
ds: A heist in Doskvol's most guarded train. Your crew has one shot at the Silver Rail's cargo. Blades' flashback mechanic means you don't need to plan ahead; you just need to be clever in the moment. Pre-generated crew. 4 players max for a tight, cinematic heist.

Explore the Sunken Fortress using Shadowdark's real-time torch mechanic — when your torch burns out, you're in actual darkness. Run by The Arcane Library team. Pre-gens. Fast char creation if yours dies. OSR dungeon crawling: resource management, clever play, the dungeon is trying to kill you.

Something is scratching at the walls of a remote Swedish farmstead. Vaesen is Nordic horror meets investigation — the Year Zero Engine makes every roll matter. Run by Free League's own team. Pre-gens. Perfect for fans of Midsommar, folklore horror, and games where violence is the last resort.

The legendary Gen Con Dungeon Delve! Teams of 4-6 compete in deadly encounters. Pre-generated level 10 characters. Tournament scoring. Top teams advance to Sunday finals. Competitive D&D at its peak — tactical mastery required. 8-hour marathon. Prizes for top 3.

THE DYING WORLD HUNGERS. You are wretches. You will probably die. Mork Borg is a doom-metal RPG where character creation takes 5 minutes and survival is not guaranteed. Bring dark humor, leave character attachment at the door.

t: The One Ring: Journey to Mirkwood
ds: A journey through the dark eaves of Mirkwood, years after the Battle of Five Armies. The One Ring captures Tolkien's tone like no other RPG. Run by Free League. Pre-gens. 5 hours for the full journey mechanics: travel, encounters, and the shadow's pull.

t: Alien RPG: Chariot of the Gods
ds: The cinematic scenario from the Alien RPG starter set. Your crew responds to a distress signal. Trust no one. The stress mechanic is a masterpiece of tension. Run by Free League. Pre-gens with hidden agendas. Saturday night + day 3 exhaustion = perfect paranoia fuel. Content: sci-fi horror, betrayal.

t: MCDM RPG: The Siege of Blackbottom
ds: The first public playtest of the MCDM RPG at Gen Con! Matt Colville's team presents a tactical fantasy RPG built from the ground up. Pre-generated heroes. All materials provided. No experience needed — this is brand new. Come shape the future of the hobby.

Starfinder Society scenario in 2nd edition rules. Digital constructs are haunting the infosphere of a space station. Tier 1-4. Bring your SFS character or use a pre-gen. Official SFS reporting. Run by Paizo's organized play team.

t: Dragonbane: Secret of the Dragon Emperor
ds: Classic fantasy with Free League's Dragonbane — spiritual successor to Sweden's first RPG. Fast, lethal, wonderfully old-school in feel but modern in design. Pre-gens. Run by Free League. Perfect Sunday morning palate cleanser after a weekend of horror.

t: Trophy Dark: The Roots That Bind
ds: A collaborative horror game where your character is doomed from the start. Trophy Dark isn't about survival — it's about how beautifully you fall apart. Characters created at the table. Narrative-first, rules-light, deeply atmospheric. Small table (4 max) for intimate horror. Thursday night for maximum atmosphere.

t: D&D Epic: The Storm King's Gambit
ds: The Gen Con 2026 D&D Epic! 20+ tables of simultaneous play, interconnected by a live narrative. Your table's choices affect every other table in real time. Pre-generated level 8 characters. The spectacle event of the convention.

Survive the frozen north in Free League's hex-crawl RPG. Forbidden Lands combines resource management, exploration, and stronghold building into a complete sandbox. Pre-gens. 5 hours for real exploration — travel, encounters, base-building. Run by Free League.

Political intrigue in the courts of the Camarilla. Pre-generated vampires with hidden loyalties and competing agendas. This is not a combat game — manipulation, alliance, and betrayal. Saturday night is the perfect slot for VtM's social intensity.
Brand new to D&D? Start here! Wizards of the Coast's trained demo team. All materials provided. Free. 2 hours.

t: Cairn: Barrow of the Elf King
ds: The designer of Cairn runs his own game. An expedition into an ancient barrow where the Elf King was buried with his living court. Cairn is rules-light, player-skill-driven, and lethal. Pre-gens. OSR at its most elegant — no to-hit rolls, just fiction and consequences. Run by the actual designer.
Cyberpunk meets Mork Borg. Jack in, get the data, get out. Probably die. Cy_Borg is fast, weird, and neon-soaked. Pre-gens provided.

Play new content from the upcoming Rage of Elements expansion! Paizo developer Luis Loza runs a special preview scenario featuring the new kineticist class. Pre-gens with new options. Your chance to play unreleased Pathfinder content and give feedback to the design team.

Cold War espionage with GURPS. Your MI6 team has 48 hours to extract a defector from East Berlin before the wall comes down. Pre-generated agents. Some GURPS familiarity helpful. Tense, realistic espionage thriller — not a dungeon crawl.

The designer of Electric Bastionland runs a session in the surreal industrial city of Bastion. You owe an impossible debt. The city is vast, weird, and full of factions. Rules explained in 5 minutes. Creative problem-solving in a world that doesn't make sense. Run by the actual designer.

Creative Director Mike Mason runs the classic Blackwater Creek. A small Vermont town harbors a terrible secret beneath its flooded landscape. Pre-gens. Call of Cthulhu at its finest — cosmic horror, investigation, and the growing realization some things cannot be stopped. Run by the line's creative director. 5 hours for a complete, devastating experience.

t: D&D: Curse of the Blood Moon
ds: A werewolf adventure! Fun for all ages!

Brandon Sanderson's Cosmere comes to tabletop RPG! Play in the worlds of Mistborn and Stormlight Archive. All materials provided. Run by Brotherwise Games. Free. No experience necessary. One of the most anticipated RPG releases of the year.

The greatest voyage in Traveller history. The Deepnight Revelation departs known space for a multi-year journey into the unknown. Pre-gens. Hard sci-fi exploration — fuel calculations, crew management, the vast emptiness between stars. Run by one of Traveller's most published authors.

t: DCC: Sailors on the Starless Sea
ds: The most famous 0-level funnel in RPG history. Each player runs 3-4 peasants against an ancient chaos cult. Most will die. Some will become heroes. DCC's gonzo energy is unmatched. Characters created at the table. Dice chain, mighty deeds, pure old-school joy.

Spy thriller meets vampire conspiracy. GUMSHOE investigation ensures you always find clues — the question is what you do with them. Pre-gens. Run by Kenneth Hite, the game's designer. Bourne Identity meets Dracula. Ken's table is one of the most sought-after seats at Gen Con.

t: Tales of the Valiant: Scarlet Citadel
ds: Explore the infamous Scarlet Citadel using Kobold Press's Tales of the Valiant rules — 5e-compatible with its own identity. Pre-gens. Run by the Kobold Press team. Multi-level, faction-rich, full of surprises.
Bar fight! Everyone gets a chair and a mug. Last one standing wins. It's D&D but just the combat. Fun for everyone!

NASCRAG's annual tournament combines tactical combat, roleplay, and puzzle-solving. Teams are scored on all three pillars. Pre-gens. NASCRAG has been running tournaments at Gen Con for over 30 years. Finalists advance to Sunday championship.

The designer of Ironsworn: Starforged runs a guided session. An ancient vault in deep space holds a relic that could change everything. Starforged's move-based system creates emergent narrative without traditional GM structure — Shawn facilitates rather than dictates. Pre-gens. Small table for deep immersion.
Shadowrun Missions Season 11 opener. A routine block party in the Barrens turns into a firefight. Bring your Missions character or use a pre-gen. Official SRM reporting. Run by Catalyst's demo team.

t: Brindlewood Bay: Murder at the Regatta
ds: Cozy mystery RPG where retired women solve murders in a quaint New England town. Think Murder She Wrote meets PbtA. The twist: the mystery isn't pre-written — the table discovers truth together. Pre-gens. Perfect Sunday wind-down.

The Gen Con PFS Special! Multi-table interactive event where your table's success or failure affects every other table in real time. The Whispering Tyrant's forces assault Gallowspire. Tier 5-16. Bring your PFS character. The highlight of organized play at Gen Con.

The JRPG-inspired TTRPG! Fabula Ultima captures the feel of Final Fantasy and Chrono Trigger. Pre-gens. Run by the designer. Dramatic villains, heroic bonds, narrative teamwork over tactical optimization. New players especially welcome.

Descend into the Heart — a nightmare dimension beneath the city of Spire. Heart is a dungeon crawl powered by consequences rather than hit points. Pre-gens. Run by co-designer Grant Howitt. Small table (4 max). Weird, beautiful, devastating fantasy that doesn't play like anything else.
Lead the Red Plume mercenaries against an orc warband threatening Hillsfar. Tactical combat focus with pre-generated level 7 characters. War-themed adventure with large-scale battle elements.

Stranger Things meets The Goonies. Something weird is happening in your small town and the adults won't listen. Kids on Bikes is collaborative storytelling with simple mechanics. Pre-gens. Great for families or anyone who loved 80s adventure movies.
